codeReviewIssues.description: Pre-existing SAFETY comment inaccuracy in free(): states 'size_of::<FreeNode>() (16 on 64-bit, 8 on 32-bit)' but FreeNode contains a single *mut pointer, so size_of is 8 on 64-bit, not 16. The invariant (min class 64 >= actual size 8) holds regardless, so no correctness impact. The AUDIT comment at line 4 correctly says '(8)'. Worker claimed 'All SAFETY comments verified accurate' but missed this discrepancy.

summary: Clean audit pass. The worker correctly identified 2 unsafe blocks + 2 unsafe fn, systematically applied the 6-category checklist, and found no bugs — an assessment I independently verify after reviewing all unsafe blocks, concurrency (IrqMutex protects all SLAB mutations), error handling (OOM paths return null/early), edge cases (size_class handles 0-size and oversized alignment), resource leaks (pages never freed — intentional), and AArch64 correctness (no inline asm). The only finding is a pre-existing SAFETY comment inaccuracy in free() that overstates FreeNode's size (says 16 on 64-bit, actually 8) — non-blocking since the safety invariant still holds. 22 existing slab tests + 5 adversarial tests provide coverage for the testable pure-logic surface (size_class). Verification: 965 tests pass, kernel builds clean, AUDIT marker present.
